questions: Was the version bump applied correctly to both package.json and package-lock.json?
Did the version bump break typecheck or build?

answers.answer: Yes. package.json line 3 now reads "version": "1.3.3" and package-lock.json lines 3 and 9 both read "version": "1.3.3". Verified via node -p "require('./package.json').version" returning 1.3.3 and direct file read of package-lock.json.
No. Both npm run typecheck (tsc --noEmit) and npm run build (tsc) exited with code 0. The dist/ directory is populated with output files.
evidence: npm run typecheck output: no errors, exit code 0
npm run build output: no errors, exit code 0
ls dist/ shows index.js, index.d.ts, and subdirectories

answers.answer: No. Only package.json and package-lock.json were modified. The edits used targeted replace operations that only changed the version field from 1.3.2 to 1.3.3.
evidence: Edit tool used on exactly two files: package.json (single replace) and package-lock.json (replace_all for the two version occurrences)
Full re-read of package.json confirms only line 3 differs from original

statement: Version bump from 1.3.2 to 1.3.3 applied successfully to package.json and package-lock.json. Typecheck and build both pass. No unrelated files were modified. The repo is ready for a v1.3.3 Git tag to trigger the existing npm publish workflow.
